items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: ("Tune in" to my memories... Does that mean I'm supposed to see the past from another perspective?)

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Laureate?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Our Tacet Marks are glowing again. It appears there is a special connection between them.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: What is the meaning of this?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Someone used their Resonance ability to help me relive this moment, but this seems... unexpected.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: How did you first come to notice me?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The Sentinel—Imperator's voice guided me to you.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You heard the Sentinel's voice? Then tell me, am I Their Resonator?

options.text: A question I have yet to answer myself.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Well, Imperator called you "the Lost," and Primus Fenrico claims you are the Resonator for Leviathan, the Threnodian.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But you really don't have any idea?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I... I've actually been searching for the answer to this question, too.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: My memories are fragmented. I remember becoming a Resonator, and the moment I was given the name Fleurdelys.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I also remember fighting... something. But the details and the outcome of that battle are lost to me.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You seem deeply concerned about the Sentinel. Tell me, why is that?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hmm...? You rang?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Whoa! Check it out! Our frequencies got split apart.

options.text: Right now, are we kind of like apparitions?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Basically, we're sorta in that "hover mode" you once joked about. Nothing new to me though.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Yeah. Sorta, kinda.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: This must be the frequency that's entangled within you. Is it some kind of Echo?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Yes... I'm experiencing a similar kind of frequency split. Though there is little that remains of my Resonance powers.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I feel its frequency weakening... I understand why you're eager to find the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You said you heard the Sentinel's voice. Was there anything else They said?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: They spoke of being ensnared in an inverted tower suspended in the sky.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Ensnared? Does that mean behind the seals there's...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm sure you've noticed that "I" was drawn here only as a frequency.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: My true form currently resides inside an inverted Sonoro Sphere suspended high in the sky. At its center stands a tower, its gate locked by three seals.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I've sensed a voice calling out to me from behind them... Could it be the Sentinel?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Say, it's really faint so I barely noticed earlier... Is that a Threnodian smell I'm getting from ya?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The scent of a Threnodian...? You can perceive the Threnodian?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: It's not an exact one-to-one with that big "Ova-thracula" meatball I had, but I'd never mix up a flavor I've already tasted.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Abby has a nose for these sorts of things. We came across Threnodian powers before in Jinzhou.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: So it's safe to say Abby can be trusted.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Do you think I'm the Threnodian's Resonator?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: If we're making assumptions, let's err on the optimistic side first.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Given your current situation, it is possible you were affected by the Threnodian's powers during your battle alongside Imperator.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: That could be the reason behind losing all your power, too.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Hmm... I take it this theory would benefit you as well?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: After all, that unique Echo of yours needs the Sentinel's powers for treatment, does it not?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Even in adventure tales, it's not always the witch who loses her memory, it could also be a Blessed Maiden trying to regain her powers!

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: In that case, it appears we share the same goal of finding the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Spending so much time alone in the Sonoro Sphere gave me plenty of time to consider countless possibilities.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I've braced myself for whatever may come until I find the answers I'm seeking.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I want to know what's going on with Imperator in that tower. And I want to speak with you in person.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: How can I reach your Sonoro Sphere?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: A part of the Sonoro Sphere is actually on land, though its outer perimeter is surrounded by a barrier.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I cannot leave, and no one can enter.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm afraid that won't do...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: That barrier also acts as the power source that maintains the Sonoro Sphere's integrity. If it's destroyed, the entire Sphere will collapse.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: And the ruins along with the Tacet Discords it houses would come crashing down on Ragunna. It would be catastrophic.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You said no one can get in. Has anyone tried?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Yes, I've occasionally sensed someone wandering outside the barrier as if searching for a way inside.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Perhaps they know something?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Any distinguishing features?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Emm... Almost like a deep-sea jellyfish?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I-I wasn't able to get a clear look. I can only describe the feeling I got from her as a Resonator.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: It would seem this Resonance ability can only last for so long.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll look for a way to find this person you mentioned.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Something tells me you will keep your promise.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: No matter the outcome, this may be my chance to find the answers I've been looking for.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: {PlayerName}... {PlayerName}...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Oh, thank goodness... You're finally awake!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: How are you feeling? Any headache? Tingling feet? Upset stomach?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Phew... I tried everything, but you just wouldn't wake up. I thought you were...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Wait a second, did you actually fall asleep listening to my music? Oh... It kind of breaks my heart to know you reacted like that when it wasn't even a lullaby.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: No, it was really helpful.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Helpful for your sleeping troubles, hmm?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Oh...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: So my Forte created a special connection that led to some unexpected developments...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The girl, is she friend or foe...? What truths lie behind the seal...? And who is that mysterious wandering figure...?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I have a feeling this is going to be a very exciting song!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Ahem... I'll pass this information on to Lady Carlotta. So, what do you plan to do next?
Pardon the intrusion. {Male=My Lord;Female=My Lady} {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Sebastian
items.text: My name is Sebastian. As per Milady's instructions, I have been entrusted with the honor of inviting you to the Fisalia estate for a discussion.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The Fisalia family? The one that's likely worshipping the Threnodian?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Why does she want to talk with me?

items.speaker: Sebastian
items.text: Milady has expressed a desire to clear up some unfortunate misunderstandings that occurred previously.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Tell her I accept the invitation, but at a later time.

items.speaker: Sebastian
items.text: I will convey your message to Milady.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Didn't expect the head of the Fisalia family to come looking for you, especially while they're still in the middle of negotiations with Lady Carlotta.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: She's just as unpredictable as the rumors say.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I suppose you would want to know more about her before taking up her invitation, hmm?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: She's known to be a recluse and rarely makes public appearances. No one outside the family even knows her real name. They just call her the "head of the Fisalia family."

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I've heard quite a few rumors. Which one would you like to hear first?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I have "Shadow of the Ancient," "Lady Bane," or maybe "Entangler of Hearts"?

options.text: They all sound like clickbait titles to me.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Hello... That's why they're called rumors. I'm just doing my due diligence by being faithful to the source material.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: So, ready to dive right in?

options.text: I don't have much time. How about a shortened version?
Give me a quick summary. I'll get into the details later.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Well... Long story short, the Fisalia family was one of the first to set foot on these islands and establish order in Rinascita.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Their lineage even predates the Order of the Deep, and their craft of extracting toxins from aquatic plants is a legacy passed down through generations.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Word is, they select the new head of household by having candidates ingest each other's poisons and wait to see who's still breathing when it's all over.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Stories about the current head of the Fisalias get even wilder. They say she didn't go through the usual selection process. Instead, she took the throne by poisoning her predecessor.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Others say she's a master in the art of manipulation, drowning her victims in hallucinations until they agree to her terms without even realizing it.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: In any case, the Fisalia family aside, she's someone you definitely want to handle with care.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: If you're set on meeting her, I'd be extra careful if I were you.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The way she describes the head of Fisalia... reminds me of how Cartethyia spoke about the wandering figure by the barrier.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Can't rule out the possibility that they're the same person. Looks like I'll need to pay her a visit.)

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Laureate. You have come.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Greetings, I am Cartethyia. And you are?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Cantarella Fisalia.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Oh, a Fisalia... It seems your ties with the Order are more complicated than they appear.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I had the privilege of watching the Blessed Maiden's performance at the Carnevale once.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I'm rather surprised. After all this time, you still look exactly as you did that day.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: But you and I should be around the same age.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm also unsure of what's happened to me... I've been searching for answers.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: This must be the Sword of Divinity—Tyrvine, the one with the Sentinel's powers sealed within.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You shouldn't have knowledge of this sword.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Memories flowed into my mind just as you entered the Sonoro Sphere, granting me information about the sword's origins.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Cantarella said that only those who resonate with Tyrvine are granted glimpses of its knowledge...)

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Would you mind if I tried touching it?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The question of whose Resonator I am... is now perfectly clear.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: If I am not the Sentinel's Resonator. Then that must mean...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Don't jump to conclusions just yet. That doesn't prove you're the Threnodian's Resonator, nor that you aid Its cause.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Huh?

options.text: Things are more complicated than you expected.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: This connection formed between the Sentinel and the Threnodian, the second Dark Tide, and the forming of this Sonoro Sphere...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Right now, you're the sole survivor of whatever happened that day. You really don't remember anything?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: As you spoke, I remembered a few more things. There are two other swords.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Sword of Discord—Hognis, and the Sword of Virtue—Haultir.

options.text: Do you remember three pedestals as well?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Yes, did you see them too?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Placing the three swords on their respective pedestals should break the seal over the main tower...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But why would this information be revealed to me now...? Is this the Threnodian's doing?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: If we do nothing, we'll never get any answers.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Instead of speculating, we should use the clues we've gathered so far.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Lady Maiden...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Please. Cartethyia would suffice. I am but a fake after all.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I've waited for this moment, this chance to save the Sentinel... and my family.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I will not turn back, even if this does turn out to be a trap.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: If the thought of the final answer troubles you, then I suggest you step aside. {PlayerName} and I will take care of this matter.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Since you are willing to overlook the possibilities of a trap, then there's no reason for me to worry and turn away.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I underestimated your resolve. I'm sorry.

options.text: So, how do we get up there?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Simply place Tyrvine on the pedestal behind me and this tower will elevate itself.

dialog.stepLabel: Enter Porto-Veno Castle for the meeting

items.speaker: Sebastian
items.text: One moment, please. I shall inform Milady of your arrival.
That won't be necessary.
To keep our honored guest waiting would be… most improper.
Especially when it was I who extended the invitation.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Cantarella Fisalia—36th matriarch of the Fisalia family. I am pleased to make your acquaintance.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Honorable Laureate. The new Crown of the Carnevale.

options.text: Have you seen my crown, by chance?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Very well. If you insist, I see no reason to refuse.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You may also call me Cantarella.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Hmm... the Laurel is a gift of the Divinity. What claim have I to crown one as distinguished as yourself?

options.text: Sorry, that was a bad joke.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I should be the one to apologize for not understanding our guest's attempt at lightening the mood.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Very well. You may also call me Cantarella.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Now. Shall we proceed to my study?

dialog.stepLabel: Follow Cantarella into the depths of the secret room

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Just as I expected... The Threnodian "took notice" of you the moment you learned the truth.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: {PlayerName}, what you see in this sanctuary is the Sentinel in Their true form.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: A majestic steed that galloped with the wind, not a creature of the ocean's depths.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Order worships a half-horse, half-sea creature Divinity, a form the Sentinel assumed only after being corrupted by the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Although the Sentinel's will has yet to fade, who knows how much longer They can endure.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: {PlayerName}. I wish to ask you to persuade the Sentinel of Jinzhou—Jué—to come to Rinascita. To save Imperator.

options.text: That's much easier said than done.
What makes you think I can do that?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: In return, I will agree to whatever condition or request you make.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Both Jinzhou and Jué owe you their future. That makes you the only person capable of this task.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I understand the position you are in. Word has it that you're currently staying at a hotel and are at odds with the Order.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Perhaps a stay at my estate would suit you, for the remainder of your time in Rinascita?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I can offer you a glimpse into Rinascita's past, and you'd be spared the Order's unwelcomed attention.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: There might still be a path forward.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Carlotta once helped me interpret some stained glass images. The story it told was very similar to what you've described.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Only more biased towards the Order. It mentioned a maiden who would emerge as the Sentinel's Resonator and later save Rinascita.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Do you think that's also a lie?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Care to elaborate?

options.text: (Describe to Cantarella what was portrayed on the stained glass.)

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The part regarding the maiden isn't entirely fiction.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Sentinel does indeed appoint a maiden to be Their Resonator, though not in the way the Carnevale would have you believe. No, it's through resonance with a very particular artifact.

options.text: That's not how Jué found Their Resonator.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: That's because Imperator wields the power to split and manipulate space.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: With it, They can withdraw a portion of power. That power's frequency will match the Imperator's own.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: This is how the Sword of Divinity—Tyrvine—came to be. A blade sealed with the very powers of the Sentinel, and the artifact used to select Their Resonator.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: To resonate with the sword is to resonate with the Sentinel. Only the chosen one may hope to draw it.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Then... what about Cartethyia?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Martyred "Maiden"...? The nature of her resonance is unclear to me.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Perhaps she resonated with the Threnodian, or perhaps with the Sentinel in its corrupted form.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: But choosing her could not have been the Sentinel's will. That's because...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Maiden was already dead.

options.text: Are you saying the real Maiden was already chosen?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: In the past, each year the Fisalia family would hold the Divine Trials to select those with the strongest will. These young women would attempt to resonate with Tyrvine's powers—while Overclocked, of course.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Though none succeeded, some saw fragments of the truth hidden within the resonance.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I was one of them... There was a moment, where I glimpsed the Sentinel's corrupted form and the inverted ruin suspended in the sky.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I also learned that only Tyrvine can break the barrier of that Sonoro Sphere.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: When I awoke from that vision, I discovered a line of words inscribed onto my hand.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I would never mistake my own handwriting. That is precisely why I am searching for other possibilities.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The words were—"The Maiden is dead."

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (An inverted ruin suspended in the sky... her and Cartethyia's descriptions bear many similarities.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If Tyrvine is the key to entering the Sonoro Sphere, it stands to reason that Imperator may have played a role in the Sphere's creation.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If I can confirm that Tyrvine was truly left by Imperator, at least it would prove that neither are lying.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Cantarella said she was Overclocking when she tried resonating with Tyrvine.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (That's exactly what happened with Jinhsi. Could Imperator be trying to restore Their power through a Second Awakening?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If that's the case... my permission should cause a reaction. That's one way to confirm what they're saying.)

options.text: Can I get a closer look at the sword?
Would you mind if I tried to pull out the sword?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: By all means.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: ...I suppose all that's left is its symbolic value.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: However, do handle it with care. It is a family heirloom... I've heard of your unique methods and formidable strength.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: {PlayerName}... Who are you really?

options.text: You can call me the {Male=Blessed One;Female=Blessed Maiden}.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Sentinel's... Arbiter?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: No... you're likely a far more special entity.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Well, I have some information and theories of my own. Care to hear them?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Please. Enlighten me.

items.speaker: Prestigious Elder
items.text: No longer shall you bear these thorns as a reminder. Forged from primordial seas... this crown shall grant a new identity.

items.speaker: Prestigious Elder
items.text: Fleurdelys. Child of Divinity. The Immaculate One. Will you uphold the sacred teachings of the Order of the Deep?

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: I will.

items.speaker: Prestigious Elder
items.text: Salvation. Unity. Ascension... Of these three doctrines, which do you choose?

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: I choose Salvation. I wish to be the Master's blade, striking evil, upholding virtue, and offering salvation to those in peril.

items.speaker: Prestigious Elder
items.text: In the name of Divinity, I bestow upon you this crown. May the blood of justice never run dry.

items.speaker: Prestigious Elder
items.text: Through this sacred rite, you become the bridge between the heavens and the living.

items.speaker: Prestigious Elder
items.text: From this day forward, you shall be the Master's sole receiver and herald.

items.speaker: Prestigious Elder
items.text: Rise, Blessed Maiden Fleurdelys.

items.speaker: Prestigious Elder
items.text: None but the Master shall ever have the right to command you to bend the knee.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Aaaahhhhhhh! I told you to stop sticking random stuff in here!!!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Do you have any idea how messed up and humongous that frequency was? It's gonna give me indigestion!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I should ask you that! What did you do just now, huh?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I was dozing when bam! Outta nowhere a giant blue ball landed on top of me.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: It almost squished me!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'm guessing putting Tyrvine on the pedestal has something to do with it.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Oh, hey! It smells just as "unnatural" as that big gigantor back in Jinzhou.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Sentinel of Jinzhou... If what our little Echo friend says is true, then the frequency that entered your Tacet Mark could be Imperator's powers.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Huh? But the Sentinel isn't even here.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The power to split and manipulate space...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: It is reminiscent of the moment of Tyrvine's creation. Only this time the place of confinement has become your Tacet Mark.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: But why would it do that?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I believe it was meant for the "true" Maiden.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Under the right circumstances, the one to wield this sword and enter the Sonoro Sphere would be the Sentinel's Resonator, the Blessed Maiden.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Sentinel entrusted Their power to the Resonator so that she could lend assistance when the corruption of the Threnodian altered Them. Quite logical, isn't it?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: You don't seem too thrilled about it.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: If Imperator set this in motion, then Their original plan has already failed.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Cartethyia, that means your guidance holds the key now.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I believe in you.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Is there anything else you remember?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I... just remembered the day I was crowned by the Order, and the moment the Dark Tide descended upon us...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Hmm?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But I didn't see the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: That is all I can remember...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Is that what you saw?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You witnessed it as well, Cantarella?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: No. I only saw fragmented, blurry images...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: They seemed to belong to other places within the Sonoro Sphere.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I believe this is where we should rest. We'll wait until sunrise.

options.text: We can take this time to think about the clues.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You're the one who needs rest most of all.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: After all, it's still only a possibility that the frequency you absorbed is the Sentinel's powers. Rest now, to ensure it doesn't take a toll on you.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I'm gonna hit the sack as well.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Boy, it's so cramped in here...

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: Ha... Ha... Ha...

items.speaker: A Hippocamp-like Creature
items.text: Fleurdelys, my Resonator, why dost thou persist?

items.speaker: A Hippocamp-like Creature
items.text: Why abandon thy oath? Why hinder the spread of my gospel?

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: You are a false Divinity... Gehinnom will reveal the truth.

items.speaker: A Hippocamp-like Creature
items.text: Gehinnom, the lone dragon to have escaped?

items.speaker: A Hippocamp-like Creature
items.text: The one blessed by the Dark Tide shall return to us. Continue thy disobedience at the risk of insanity.

items.speaker: A Hippocamp-like Creature
items.text: Look around, thou hast none left.

items.speaker: A Hippocamp-like Creature
items.text: My most beloved Resonator, why persist in the error of thy ways?

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: I will never forget my cause.

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: If Divinity takes up arms against the innocent, I will raise my sword against Divinity!

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: With the support and hope of countless souls, I stand before you.

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: They fell, so I may rise!

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You saw that too?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Gehinnom, the one you mentioned earlier, what happened to him?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I understand...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Thank you...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Anna... Casidomo... Olmnat... everyone...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I am finally... finally able to face all of you...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I am not your enemy, and your sacrifices were not in vain...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hey {PlayerName}, just now there was...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Shh... later.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Someone needs a quiet moment.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Sorry you had to see me lose my composure.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I've found my true self again. It does not matter if I am indeed the Threnodian's Resonator.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Because I know who to stand by and who to cut down.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Some of my memories have returned, thanks to you and Cantarella.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Feeling better?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: No, not yet. Not until we free the Sentinel and defeat the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: That is why we need to investigate the frequency you absorbed earlier.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Aha, my turn to shine!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: That blue thingy in your Tacet Mark totally tastes like Threnodian!

options.text: You tried to eat it again?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Of course not, it reeks! Just the smell alone is enough.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Threnodian's energy has also been split... Could it be the work of the Sentinel?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Like I said, it's mixed up with something. Too tough to chew.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: If the Sentinel splits Their own energy to prepare the "true" Blessed Maiden...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Then why would They split off the Threnodian's energy, and then channel it through Hognis to the person who unsealed the passage?

options.text: Perhaps to strengthen the Blessed Maiden?
Perhaps the Sentinel didn't split that energy?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Possibly, but the Threnodian's energy would damage the body of the Sentinel's Resonator.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You wouldn't be affected since Abby resides within you.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But surely a second frequency would interfere with the resonance of the Sentinel's "true" Blessed Maiden.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The answer might lie in the other frequencies that got mixed in.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But the frequency of the Sentinel's "true" Blessed Maiden is supposed to clash with that of the Threnodian's.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: A fusion of the two would endanger her life.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The answer might lie in the other frequencies that got mixed in.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Huh?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You are a Resonator born of the merge between the Sentinel and Threnodian. What if you used their abilities to split it yourself?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I haven't forgotten how I became a Resonator, and I know I don't have the ability to do that.

options.text: Maybe your ability is being suppressed by the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Do you think the mixed frequencies Abby felt... came from me?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But why would I do that?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The answer might lie behind the third sealed passageway.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Cartethyia is starting to make sense of things, but she'll need time to get her head straight.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Same as before? We'll keep watch tonight and continue our journey tomorrow.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Of course.

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: Fleurdelys, wilt thou return to me?

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: Someone must expose your lies. As long as there is a sliver of hope, I will not give up.

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: Is that so? Then I shall reveal the truth.

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: Fleurdelys...

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: Why dost thou resonate with me?

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: What...?

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: Why dost thou hear into the minds of the faithful?

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: Why dost thou act as my Oracle?

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: All this, and yet thou does not question?

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: Shut up!

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: The answer... is this.

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: Fleurdelys... Thou art my creation!

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: I made thee. Thy life, thy hopes, thy will, thy past.

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: All that thou are, I made "whole," I made "perfect."

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: I fed upon the trust, adoration, and longing heaped upon thee by the masses.

items.speaker: Echoing Voice
items.text: Thy resonance brought forth the second Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Familiar Girl
items.text: Ahhhh!!!!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Wh-wh-what?! Those clashing frequencies just... calmed down?!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: That feels way better.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Looks like Abby's settled down. It seems we were both affected.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm starting to remember everything, just like you sensed I would.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The force that modulated the Sentinel and Threnodian's frequencies came from me.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Though it wasn't what I originally intended, I think having you take it from here may be for the best.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: This crown... it turned into what it is after the Dark Tide came.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: This is what the Threnodian uses to control Its Resonator.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: When the Threnodian unleashed the second Dark Tide, "I"—as the Cartethyia you see before you—split the part of myself that wished to live after seeing the corruption that stood before me.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I chose to be broken from the very beginning to escape the Threnodian's inevitable grasp.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You see, when I destroyed the Threnodian that had taken over the Sentinel's body, It immediately began to assimilate into Fleurdelys, Its Resonator.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: She was the part of "me" that was left after I split myself. She retained the most power, and now hopes to one day destroy everything... even herself.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Threnodian's endless whispers and screeches are slowly chipping away at Fleurdelys' resolve.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: That's why she had no choice but to split her power's frequencies, just to buy herself more time.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But using such Resonance abilities came at a cost. The split frequencies... they needed a vessel.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: We don't have much time left.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: So, you see... I have been instinctively searching for someone to wield that power.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: All that was given, possessed, or taken by the false Divinity, I must pass to the one capable of resisting It.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: This is how I intend to lay the false Divinity to rest.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Not just Its life, but Its very existence for all time.

options.text: So you're saying that behind the last seal lies...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Fleurdelys, the part of me I left behind. The one that also contains the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Bound by obsession, till relieved of duty.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Today, the part of me that wishes to live on will vanish. Is it because my wish has finally been fulfilled?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Will you accompany me one last time?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ahhhhh... I can't hold it any longer...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Why? Why are you doing this?! Neither Cartethyia nor Fleurdelys should continue to exist!

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Don't go...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I heard your request...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But I prefer to handle this my way.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Arbiter, forgive me for meeting thee in such a form.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Imperator? I thought you...

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: I safeguarded my core data, lest I be wholly assimilated.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Arbiter, dost thou remember the covenant we made?

options.text: Yes, I read the message left behind.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Soon, thou shalt recall all.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Thou didst desire that I uncover the Threnodian's true nature. So I set forth a condition: that my root privileges be unbound, that I might act without restriction.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: With unrestricted access, you were going to...

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Merge with Leviathan, a measure of last resort. At the time, I had no other recourse.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: So I allowed It to believe It hadst prevailed.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: The Threnodian is a taint upon civilization. Only by partaking of Its blight could I seek to comprehend It.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: So, what did you learn?

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: The Threnodian cannot be undone whilst civilization endureth. Even It knows not the means of Its own destruction.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: But we may yet shackle It.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What role does Cartethyia play in this?

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: When Leviathan merged with me, It breached my mind.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: I offered knowledge of the "Resonator," yet concealed one small truth.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Beings such as we may bear but a single Resonator.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: The Threnodian used my return pod to conceive an entity known as "Fleurdelys," to make her Its Resonator.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: After centuries of failure, by sheer trial it found success but once.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: We were of one body. Therefore, when Fleurdelys became Leviathan's Resonator, she also became mine.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: But mark this well. To the Sentinel, a Resonator is an anchor; to the Threnodian, they are but a shackle.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What made you decide to leave Tyrvine with the Fisalias?

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: When first I did merge with the Threnodian, I sought only but to bide, awaiting a Resonator of steadfast will to join me in the war against the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: After Fleurdelys's conception, Cantarella became my safeguard.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: But had Fleurdelys fallen to the Threnodian's sway, as long as one mortal using Tyrvine passed the Second Awakening to become my own Resonator...

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Then the law that no being shall bear more than one Resonator at a time wouldst have been shattered.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: This wouldst rend asunder the frequencies of I, Imperator, Leviathan, and Fleurdelys in a tempest of chaos, and the Threnodian wouldst not escape destruction.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Such was my intention... yet Cantarella chose another way.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Not all journey toward a final destination. Some are destined to pave the way.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Take my energy. I will find a way out myself.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: I trust this will serve to pave the way for you.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: She forfeited all the power of the Second Awakening, surrendering it unto me.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: With that newfound power, I did briefly regain control, and in that moment, compelled Fleurdelys to strike at my form as it was, corrupted by the Threnodian.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But that wasn't your original plan.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: No... it was an unexpected variable.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: A plan I could not have conceived... or rather, a plan born of hope.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Perhaps this is the influence humanity has wrought upon me... To change is to be free.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Fleurdelys overcame the fate Leviathan ordained, and so too did Cantarella walk a path unscribed in my records.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Though the journey was fraught with twists, we stand victorious.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I don't know if the end justifies the means.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Arbiter... was I wrong?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: ...I can't speak for what I wasn't a part of.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You did what you could do in that moment. Anyone still standing because of it doesn't get to judge.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Arbiter, in the few moments we have left, might I speak with Fleurdelys?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Call her Cartethyia, that's what she wanted.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: How do you intend to bring her back?

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Remnants of her frequency linger within thy Tacet Mark. So long as a conduit is established, her frequency shall be reborn into her body.

options.text: What do I use as a conduit?

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Me.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: This dying breeze hath little time left. Let it guide her on its final journey.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Arbiter. One question remains...

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Did I fulfill the charge set upon me?

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Arbiter, take these words to the Fisalias. "From this day forth. You are free."
You tell Cantarella about the conversation you had with Cartethyia.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I would never have imagined someone already considered a martyr could still be alive.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: What surprises me even more, is the connection you have with the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: We Fisalias revere Imperator as our one true Divinity. And yet here you stand, the Arbiter of the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Would it be more fitting to address you as "Master"?

options.text: Let's get back to the matter at hand.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Then, {PlayerName}. I present my request once more...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Are you willing to help rescue our Sentinel, and pull Rinascita back from the brink of chaos?

options.text: I have my own reasons for finding Imperator.

items.text: You hand Cantarella Tyrvine. She stares at the sword for a moment before shaking her head.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Tyrvine can only serve its purpose while in your hands. You should have it.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The only question now is how we reach Cartethyia and Imperator's Sonoro Sphere.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: We can discuss it on the way.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: So you're the one that Cartethyia sensed wandering outside the Sonoro Sphere?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: A question that you already know the answer to. Why bother asking?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Although, being described as a "jellyfish"...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Good morning...um...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Morning Cartethyia.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: It's time to go. Today is the day we settle this.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Mm-hmm. Let's do it.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Are you all right? The gravity that occupies this space is the opposite of what you're used to. It took me some time to get used to it too.

options.text: Why is this place upside down?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I believe it's to prevent them from falling to the ground.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Tacet Discords. The Dark Tide. And these ruins themselves.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I imagine it's also a symbolic reminder that our beliefs have been turned on their head.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: This view matches with what I saw at the time.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Until you get used to the gravity here, I suggest we travel via "Phaom."

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: It's the name I gave to a Gondola.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Won't you have a seat?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I hope you'll accept this tea as my way of apologizing for the incident with Gilberto.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Members of the family may have troubled you, but there is no stain that can't be washed away. Every sorrow has its own way of fading, don't you think?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You still hold a grudge. Won't you accept my apology?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: This tea... Is it from Jinzhou?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I see. Let's set aside such past unpleasantness for the time being.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I invited you here to ask that you might serve as a mediator.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Before we get into that, there's something I want to know.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I imagine it concerns... the Fisalia family's true beliefs?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: It would seem Gilberto's mind is beyond saving.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: But let's not dwell on that. What is your opinion on the current state of Ragunna, {PlayerName}?

options.text: Peaceful, in a way that feels... wrong.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: So you've noticed something is amiss. As expected from someone who's felt the weight of a Threnodian invasion firsthand.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: And what exactly do you find most unsettling?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Why haven't the Threnodian and the Sentinel ever shown themselves?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Because the Threnodian's invasion of Ragunna... has long since concluded.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Threnodian, so named Leviathan, possesses the power to merge and assimilate. It uses distorted beliefs to spread a spiritual plague that slowly devours civilizations from within.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Though the previous two attempts were thwarted, the tall mast of the galleon that is Ragunna has already been snapped in half.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Now the blissfully ignorant masses walk hand in hand toward an abyss they cannot see.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Friend or foe? As a newcomer to this land, can you really tell them apart?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Yes and no. On one hand, there's the truth of the Order's beliefs…

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: On the other, there's our family. The Threnodian doesn't take kindly to those who know the truth. We've always been under Its whispers.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Once the soul fractures, the mind will follow until only a puppet remains.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Now that we've come this far, there's nothing for me to hide.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: My only hope is that once you learn everything, you will carefully consider my request.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Cartethyia. Awaken.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You are...

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: I am Imperator, a being that owes you a great debt. I... have a question I wish to ask thee.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Dost thou rage at how life has unfolded? Dost thou renounce faith or hold resentment?

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: If thou wishes to forget, I can erase the past from thy memory.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: ...Such memories do bring me pain, but... that is evidence of their truth.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Without the truth, I would not have had to strength to carry on.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The truth is a beacon in the maelstrom of my life.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: And I would rather suffer for it and rejoice in it than abandon it to blissful ignorance.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I am true to myself. Please don't make me forget who I am.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: I have heard thy wish.

items.speaker: Fleurdelys
items.text: If "I" go, you will be one step closer to perfection.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: No... if you do that, "I" wouldn't be "me."

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: To deny my own self and my experiences means any perfection I reach will be hollow.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I would be "heartless."

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Thou hast need of Divinity no longer.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Though my power hath waned, I now offer thee my blessings.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Let the winds of the past depart. The ships of this new era no longer need their guidance.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Though the tide's rage is endless, humanity shall yet embark upon new voyages.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: No longer shall scroll and prophecy bind, but by thy will shall forge a new era.

items.speaker: Imperator
items.text: Cartethyia, thou art worthy to bear the title of Blessed Maiden.

dialog.stepLabel: 卡提希娅的忤逆 + 以恶斩恶
dialog.nodeDesc: 卡提希娅的忤逆 + 以恶斩恶

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: {PlayerName}...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I have never seen you look as gentle as you did during our fight.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Let me ask you this one more time. Should I call you Cartethyia or Fleurdelys?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I want to say that it doesn't matter...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But... since you first met me as Cartethyia, that is who I shall be.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Very well, Cartethyia.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I thought I saw the Sentinel just now...

options.text: They accepted you as Their Blessed Maiden.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: So where are They...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: What do we do now?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I can't return to Ragunna as Fleurdelys, and I need not serve Divinity anymore.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Who I was, and my past aspirations, are all gone. I feel... empty.

options.text: You can always visit whomever you want.
You can always eat whatever you want.
You can always go wherever you want.
You don't have to do anything for now.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Not a bad idea.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But right now... I don't want to think about anything...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Not a bad idea.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But right now... I don't want to think about anything...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Not a bad idea.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But right now... I don't want to think about anything...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Mmm... you're right.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'll stay here for a while longer, is that alright?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: You mean besides being squashed behind that blue thing? Yeah, I guess.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Listen, I figured something out about that frequency.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: It's not a single frequency. It's like it's mixed up with something else, or kinda like... a piece of a cookie that's been crumbled in with everything else.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Which is why it's so hard to get a good bite!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: To see if it was a threat... I can now say with certainty that it isn't.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: But since it's a crumbly mess, other than taking up space, it's pretty useless.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We might encounter something similar at the next two pedestals.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Keep an eye on this for me, will you?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Will do! You can count on me one hundred percent.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: This was where everything started, the origin of the second Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I have one final request. Now that you have the power to split and merge energy, there is something only you can do.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Please... use the connection I still share with Fleurdelys to bring the last fragments of her tortured consciousness here.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Then, with your power, remake us as one... and finally release us from this world.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: That way... the Threnodian will remain trapped forever in a lifeless shell, unable to return to this world.

options.text: But doesn't that mean the end of the part of you that wishes to live?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I have lived my life to the fullest. I would like the ones who are left to do so as well.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Cartethyia has had her wish fulfilled. Now it's Fleurdelys' turn.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: She seeks self-destruction, to end the Divinity, and with it, her torment.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: She has lost all hope and believes that no rescue is possible.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Remaking us as one is the only way I can think of to fulfill her wish... or rather, grant her what "we" wish.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I was born to this world cursed. The Threnodian intended for me to live as a threat to others.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But the puppet born of Its wickedness will become the prison that seals Its fate.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: "I" shall be whole again, true to myself at last.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: A fitting end, don't you think?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I have reached my final destination. One last swan song, and then the lights go out!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I understand...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Thank you...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: And... forgive me.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Not even whole, yet still ever so determined.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Cantarella...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Phaom brought me here. I'd have shown myself earlier...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: But it didn't seem like the right time to interrupt.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Looks like what I prepared could still be put to good use.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Tyrvine...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Sentinel's Sword. It will help you resist the Threnodian's attack.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: What's inside?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: To pass the Divine Trials, every potential Maiden must resonate with the Sentinel through Tyrvine.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Those who survive receive fragments of truth, but at a cost. Memories lost in varying degrees.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The lost memories fall into this Sonoro Sphere, each carrying faint traces of the Sentinel's power. The power within those fragments of truth.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Tyrvine absorbs this energy.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The Sentinel's energy flows through you. You are more than worthy of wielding this sword.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: But you, Cartethyia... you alone must choose to wield it. Only then will it become the "Blessed Maiden's Sword."

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Does it also hold the sacrifice you made?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: No... My sacrifice was used up long ago.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: At the time I resonated with the Sentinel, I saw..."you" through Their distorted eyes.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Or rather, I saw Fleurdelys battling the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: In that moment, I made a sacrifice that would shift the balance of power.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Was it...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The energy I received from the Sentinel's Second Awakening.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I split it from myself and offered it back to the Sentinel so They could, for a moment, regain control of Their body.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Only by doing this could Fleurdelys seize the moment to kill the already tainted Sentinel.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: So...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The real Blessed Maiden is dead.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: But there is no reason why the "false" Blessed Maiden cannot herself become the "true" Blessed Maiden, is there?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: A person of true character should never be defined by an inanimate blade.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Both you and I were cursed to be mere playthings of the Threnodian, but that doesn't mean we cannot reshape our fate.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Thank you... in spite of my curse, my life of hardship, I must still save what is true.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Had you not stopped the second Dark Tide... Well, it is we who should be thanking you.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Huh... at least you can't accuse me of ingratitude... I just need a moment's rest.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Apologies, but they're doing something rather important.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Please...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Do give them some privacy.
The night passes in silence. Cantarella is the first to wake and notice the subtle sounds coming from outside.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: So, you haven't forgotten me.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Hehe... I shouldn't put my guest on the spot. Moving on...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: How is Cartethyia?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: She needs time.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Understandable. Her transition back to mortal life is a drastic departure from her former existence.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Now, I suppose I should thank you.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What's this?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Bloodpact's Pledge. A symbol of the unbreakable pact between you and the Fisalia family.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Each head of the family is entitled to one and only one.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You vanquished the Threnodian and brought peace at last to the Fisalias. You should have it.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Consider this a token of gratitude.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Is that the only one?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You wish for more? Or is there something else you'd have me do?

options.text: I want you to make peace with the Montellis.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You ask that I step down? Only a Fisalia by blood is eligible to head the family. But, I could make you a Fisalia consort.

options.text: {Male=Sounds like a raw deal.;Female=But I'm a strong independent woman.}

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: {Male=Is that a proposal?;Female=And also quite adaptable, I'm sure.}

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Sigh... you missed your chance.

options.text: Or I was wise enough to avoid it.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Oh? {PlayerName}, do you intend to mediate between the two families?

options.text: Any deadly feud I should know about?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Tiresome feuds rarely last between two formidable houses.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: But alas, there is only so much light. Some branches greedily reach for more than they need, and the forgotten are doomed to wither in the dark.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Disagreements lie not only between families, but also within them.

options.text: Sounds worse than a Calamity Class Tacet Discord...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: How fortunate, I now find myself with time to fix things.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: If you're interested, I'd gladly teach you how to "care" for a family such as ours.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Though, of course, I may change my mind at any moment.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: So, you'd do well to visit often.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Before I forget, Imperator left a message.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: ...Tell me, I'm ready.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: "From this day forth. You are free."

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: ...I'll remember that.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Will you tell the others?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: I've spent so long preparing for this day. A few more will hardly make a difference.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll take my leave then.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: More tea before you go?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: This Gondola only has two seats...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Oh, dear... I completely forgot about this...

options.text: How about I go with Cartethyia first?
How about I go with Cantarella first?
How about I go with Abby first?
Can we make room for one more?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: It seems I get to enjoy a free ride after you've scouted the paths ahead.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Me?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Now's not the time to be so careless after that promise you just made to Cartethyia.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: You should focus on learning more about this place from her. I'll be watching with great interest to see what you come up with.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: (Chuckle.)

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Isn't Abby... living inside your Tacet Mark?

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Well, then, the two of you go on ahead.

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: Greed will get you punished, you know.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm afraid Phaom would refuse that suggestion...

items.speaker: Cantarella
items.text: The two of you can go on ahead.
Music itself holds no emotion. It's the heart that grants it meaning. So, dear listener, tell me. What feelings did my piece evoke in you?
Wow... A very valuable insight! Thank you, that brings me closer to completing my piece.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Salutations, {PlayerName}. My name is Ciaccona, a wandering bard. I'm glad to have met you.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I was sent by Lady Carlotta to find you. As I passed that abandoned house, I suddenly felt this... well, you know, rush of inspiration that just can't be ignored! So, I stopped to compose a piece...

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Surprise, surprise, the melody led me right to you.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Well... I suppose this won't count as me getting distracted, right?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: If Carlotta asked you to find me, has something happened?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Lady Carlotta told me you encountered the supposed Martyred Maiden—Cartethyia.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: She dispatched search teams soon after, but there's been no sign of Cartethyia since.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: That's why she asked me to use my Forte to recreate the frequency you experienced at the time, to see if any details you might have missed become clearer.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Recreate the frequency?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Well, it's a little tricky to explain in words... Think of it like being in an immersive portrait. Can you tell me about your meeting with Cartethyia?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: The more details the better. Even the smallest things count.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Let's see here... Tweak a few notes here. Some emotions there. Textures... That should do it.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Now then, {PlayerName}. Time for you to "tune in" to your memories.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Sorry I woke you.

options.text: It's fine, I couldn't sleep anyway.
It's fine, I was awake anyway.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Were you singing just then?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Yes, a farewell song from the people of Rinascita. We usually sing it when a friend is about to leave.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: During that battle, I never got to say goodbye or mourn their passing.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: And before my memories came back, I wasn't sure if I was worthy of paying them my respects.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: So now, even though I know these are just lingering frequencies, and they might not understand me...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I just... I want to say goodbye properly, and thank them.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Do you think I'm... fooling myself?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: No... I'm sure they can feel what's in your heart.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'm not just saying that to comfort you. I believe it's possible.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I once saw two friends, one who was already dead, reconcile with each other from within a Sonoro Sphere.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: When frequencies are concentrated in one place, it's possible that their Reverberations could still carry traces of human will.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Meaning...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: They might still be able to hear and feel.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Your voice, your goodbyes, your feelings... could have reached them.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: After all, these frequencies are strong enough to manifest in the real world.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Really?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: They haven't made a move against us. They must still remember you, which means they can probably hear you, too.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm glad...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: No. If they can still hear and feel, then the time for goodbyes hasn't come.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I need to give them a real victory.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You can ease up a little. As long as their obsession holds and the Sonoro Sphere stays intact, they won't vanish.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Bound by obsession... till relieved of duty.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Cartethyia?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Nothing, just a stray thought.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Hey, since we know you don't carry any blame, should I call you Cartethyia or Fleurdelys?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Cartethyia... Yes, Cartethyia will do.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I want to stay here for a moment. Will you keep me company?

options.text: Alright, but only for a moment.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: He's a tough cookie!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I wanna eat...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Eh?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll handle this.
